research if opencode has hooks that we could use to hook pc into it like we do with claude code

## Yes ‚Äî opencode is hookable, but the integration shape is different from Claude Code

**Short version:** opencode has a plugin/hook system rich enough to host `pc`'s core flows (inject + capture), but the primitives differ in two important ways: (1) there's no clean `UserPromptSubmit`-with-`additionalContext` equivalent ‚Äî you inject via `experimental.chat.messages.transform` instead; and (2) hooks are **JS/TS functions in a plugin module**, not shell commands, so `pc` (a Rust binary) can't be wired in directly via a settings file the way it is for Claude Code/Codex. You'd ship a thin JS plugin shim that shells out to the `pc` binary.

### How `pc` hooks into Claude Code today (baseline)
- `UserPromptSubmit` ‚Üí `inject` (prepend cited briefing, prompt-aware, synchronous)
- `Stop` ‚Üí `capture` (distill transcript ‚Üí wiki)
- `PostToolUse` ‚Üí awareness deltas surfaced mid-turn as `<system-reminder>`
- `SessionStart` / `SessionEnd` ‚Üí open-questions + cleanup
- reads transcripts from `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl`

### Mapping to opencode

| `pc` need | opencode hook | Viable? |
|---|---|---|
| **inject** (read user prompt, inject relevant wiki context *before* the LLM responds) | `experimental.chat.messages.transform` ‚Äî mutate the messages array; the latest user message is in the array, so you can read it and `push` a briefing | ‚úÖ but **experimental** |
| **capture** (distill after the turn settles) | `event` hook filtering `session.idle` | ‚úÖ |
| read transcript/history | `ctx.client` SDK (`client.session.*`) or read opencode's on-disk session storage directly (same pattern as reading `~/.claude/projects`) | ‚úÖ |
| open-questions on session start | `event` ‚Üí `session.created` | ‚úÖ |
| **awareness deltas mid-turn** (PostToolUse ‚Üí `<system-reminder>`) | `tool.execute.after` can *observe* tool calls but **cannot inject context** | ‚öÝÔ∏è Partial ‚Äî fold deltas into the next `messages.transform` instead of injecting mid-turn |

### Two gotchas worth flagging
1. **Don't use `experimental.chat.system.transform` for inject.** It does *not* include the user's message text ‚Äî a feature request to add it was **closed as not-planned** ([#27401](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/27401), [#17637](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/17637)). That makes prompt-aware injection impossible through it. `messages.transform` is the right hook because it sees the full message array including the current prompt. A real plugin ([opencode-dynamic-context-pruning](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning)) already does production message-array rewriting this way, so the approach is proven.
2. **No direct binary invocation.** Claude Code spawns `pc` as a shell command from `settings.json`; Codex does the same via `~/.codex/config.toml`. opencode runs JS/TS plugin functions instead. The bridge: a small plugin in `.opencode/plugins/` (or `~/.config/opencode/plugins/`) that uses `ctx.$` (Bun shell) to exec the `pc` binary ‚Äî pass the prompt/messages on stdin, splice `pc`'s stdout into the messages array. Same "shell out to the real engine" pattern as the Codex toml hooks, just wrapped in a JS shim.

### Stability caveat
The injection path depends on `experimental.*` hooks. opencode is actively reworking this area ‚Äî a dedicated pre-inference hook (`chat.request.before`) was requested and folded into ongoing discussion ([#21240](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/21240), dup of #19425). So a `pc`‚Üîopencode bridge is feasible **now**, but expect the inject hook's exact name/signature to churn until those land non-experimentally.

**Bottom line:** feasible and worth a spike. Build it as a JS plugin shim ‚Üí `pc` binary, with `messages.transform` for inject and the `event` hook (`session.idle`) for capture. Accept that mid-turn awareness deltas degrade to next-turn injection, and pin to the experimental API with the expectation of churn.

Sources:
- [opencode Plugins docs](https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/)
- [Plugin hooks reference gist (johnlindquist)](https://gist.github.com/johnlindquist/0adf1032b4e84942f3e1050aba3c5e4a)
- [Plugin dev guide (rstacruz)](https://gist.github.com/rstacruz/946d02757525c9a0f49b25e316fbe715)
- [Issue #21240 ‚Äî pre-inference hook request](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/21240)
- [Issue #27401 ‚Äî expose user message in system.transform (not planned)](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/27401)
- [opencode-dynamic-context-pruning plugin](https://github.com/Opencode-DCP/opencode-dynamic-context-pruning)

Created: **https://github.com/pablof7z/proactive-context/issues/2** ‚Äî "Hook pc into opencode via its plugin system" (labeled `enhancement`).

It captures the full research: the hook mapping table, the two gotchas (use `messages.transform` not `system.transform`; plugins are JS/TS so you need a shell-out shim to the `pc` binary), the experimental-API stability caveat, the proposed spike, and all sources.
